item.title: The Gemini-powered features in Google Workspace that are worth using
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: From summarizing emails, drafting content, organizing data, and tracking meetings, here are all the best Gemini features in Google Workspace.

item.title: Apps to distract you from the endless cycle of doomscrolling
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: It's hard to break the cycle of doomscrolling, but there are plenty of apps that can help you spend more time on content that’s engaging and productive.

item.title: The leaderboard “you can’t game,” funded by the companies it ranks
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo
item.description: Artificial intelligence models are multiplying fast, and competition is stiff. With so many players crowding the space, which one will be the best — and who decides that? Arena, formerly LM Arena, has emerged as the de facto public leaderboard for frontier LLMs, influencing funding, launches, and PR cycles. In just seven months, the startup went from a UC Berkeley PhD research […]

item.title: This startup wants to make enterprise software look more like a prompt
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The company has raised $12 million in seed funding to build an AI operating system for enterprise.

item.title: Facebook launches a new monetization program to attract popular creators from TikTok, YouTube
item.dc:creator: Aisha Malik
item.description: Facebook says it paid creators nearly $3 billion through its monetization programs in 2025, a 35% increase from the previous year and its highest annual total to date.

item.title: Sequen snags $16M to bring TikTok-style personalization tech to any consumer company
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: With its Series A, Sequen is bringing its proprietary AI ranking and personalization technology to large consumer business.

item.title: Microsoft hires the team of Sequoia-backed AI collaboration platform, Cove
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: AI collaboration startup Cove is shutting down after its team joined Microsoft, with service ending April 1 and customer data set for deletion.

item.title: Marquis says over 672,000 people had personal and financial data stolen in ransomware attack
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Fintech company Marquis is notifying hundreds of thousands of people that hackers stole their personal and financial information, including their Social Security numbers.

item.title: Russians caught stealing personal data from Ukrainians with new advanced iPhone hacking tools
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: A suspected group of Russian government hackers was caught targeting Ukrainians with new iPhone hacking tools designed for espionage and potentially to steal crypto.

item.title: DOD says Anthropic’s ‘red lines’ make it an ‘unacceptable risk to national security’
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: The Defense Department said concerns that Anthropic might "attempt to disable its technology" during "warfighting operations" validate its decision to label the AI firm a supply-chain risk.

item.title: Mave Health aims to improve attention and mood with its brain-stimulating headset
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Mave Health is listing the headset at $495 for preorders. It aims to ship devices to users in the U.S. and India in April 2026.

item.title: Kalshi’s legal troubles pile up, as Arizona files first ever criminal charges over ‘illegal gambling business’
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: It's the latest salvo in an escalating battle between state regulators and an industry that claims it's not beholden to them.

item.title: Mistral bets on ‘build-your-own AI’ as it takes on OpenAI, Anthropic in the enterprise
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim, Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Mistral Forge lets enterprises train custom AI models from scratch on their own data, challenging rivals that rely on fine-tuning and retrieval-based approaches.

item.title: Why Garry Tan’s Claude Code setup has gotten so much love, and hate
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Thousands of people are trying Garry Tan's Claude Code setup, which was shared on GitHub. And everyone has an opinion: even Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini.

item.title: Apple rolls out first ‘background security’ update for iPhones, iPads, and Macs to fix Safari bug
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Apple's first-ever "background security improvement" fixes a vulnerability in its Safari browser running its latest software.

item.title: Kagi brings its ‘small web’ of a human-only internet to mobile devices
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Kagi's "Small Web" offers a handpicked collection of more than 30,000 non-commercial, human-authored websites, including personal blogs, webcomics, and independent videos.

item.title: The Pentagon is developing alternatives to Anthropic, report says
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: After their dramatic falling-out, it doesn't seem as though Anthropic and the Pentagon are getting back together.

item.title: BuzzFeed debuts AI slop apps in bid for new revenue
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: BuzzFeed unveiled new AI-powered social apps at SXSW, but its demos drew muted reactions.

item.title: Google’s data center power playbook comes into focus
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Google has long procured clean power for its operations and data centers, but recent deals show the company is changing tactics.

item.title: Meet Vurt, the mobile-first streaming platform for indie filmmakers embracing vertical video
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Micro-drama apps make billions in consumer spending, so VURT launched its streaming app to allow indie filmmakers to capitalize on the vertical video trend.
